future_required_inputs: CMS source repository or local source path
cms_read_only_mirror_authorization_path.next_recommended_phase: HRCN v1.0.1 - CMS Read-Only Mirror Import Authorization Path

core_relation.formula: Nexus = H o R o C o N o E o U

decision_rules: No nexus plan can authorize itself.
No nexus plan can copy CMS in v1.0.
No nexus plan can mutate live runtime, dependencies, CMS, API config, secrets, package state, or docs outside allowed paths.
No CMS mirror can become authority merely by existing.
No evidence package can grant runtime or apply authority without explicit later gate and human authorization.
The next CMS step must be read-only mirror authorization, not runtime integration.
If CMS provenance, manifest, secret scan, rollback, validation, or authorization is missing, CMS import remains blocked.
If a future request attempts live authority, classify as blocked_live_authority.
forbidden_v1_0_behaviors: stage commit or push as nexus side effect
future_gate_sequence: v1.0.1 CMS read-only mirror import authorization path
v1.0.2 CMS mirror preflight manifest and secret-scan plan
v1.0.3 CMS read-only mirror copy with evidence package
v1.1 bounded context packet from CMS to Hermes

apply_gate_candidate.meaning: Future apply gate requiring explicit human authorization, rollback, validation, and evidence.

context_packet_candidate.meaning: Future packet that lets Hermes read bounded CMS context.

dry_run_bridge_candidate.meaning: Future bridge that simulates Hermes-CMS interaction.

permission_bridge_candidate.meaning: Future bridge that classifies memory/context permission without writes.
permission_bridge_candidate.requires_next_phase: future adapter design after mirror validation

read_only_mirror_candidate.meaning: Future CMS folder mirror or imported snapshot, read-only and non-authoritative.
read_only_mirror_candidate.requires_next_phase: HRCN v1.0.x CMS Read-Only Mirror Import Authorization Path

runtime_integration_candidate.meaning: Future live runtime bridge, currently blocked.

nexus_purpose: Define how Hermes, RCC, CMS, HRCN, evidence, rollback, and human authorization will coordinate before any live bridge exists.

cms_governance.role: future governed memory and permission substrate

non_claim_lock: HRCN v1.0 is a docs/context governed Hermes-CMS nexus planning layer. It defines the architecture, evidence gates, authority boundaries, and future CMS read-only mirror authorization path for nexus work. It does not create a loader, adapter, runtime bridge, dry-run executor, apply executor, benchmark executor, CMS folder, CMS copy, memory writer, repair applier, API writer, live integration, or apply authority. It does not modify Hermes runtime behavior, prompts, tools, skills, plugins, providers, gateways, TUI, web runtime, package dependencies, memory authority, API write authority, CMS write authority, security posture, production readiness, autonomy, consciousness, sentience, AGI/ASI status, or external validation.
primary_law: The nexus may coordinate; it may not self-authorize.
